source: AD&D 2nd Edition Planescape Campaign Setting, Al-Qadim Campaign Setting, Dark Sun Campaign Setting, Spelljammer, Complete Book of Humanoids (PHBR10), various sourcebooks
description: Non-PHB player character races available in specific campaign settings. Note: availability depends on campaign setting and DM approval.
usage_note: Most of these races are setting-specific. Players should consult their DM before selecting these races. Level limits may be applied depending on the campaign.

planar_races.description: Races from the Planescape Campaign Setting — beings touched by the planes of existence

aasimar.description: Humans with celestial bloodlines; blessed by good-aligned outer planes
special_abilities: Resistance to electricity and acid (save at +2, half damage even on fail)
Darkvision 60 ft (superior infravision)
Once per day: Daylight (as Light spell but radius 20 ft, duration 1 hour)
+2 to saves vs. death magic and poison
Detect evil 3/day (as Know Alignment for evil beings only)
Cannot willingly commit evil acts without penalty: lose special abilities for 1 month
special_disadvantages: Stand out as unusual; -2 to Hide checks in urban environments
Fiends specifically target them for corruption

tiefling.description: Humans with fiendish bloodlines; tainted by evil outer planes
special_abilities: Resistance to fire (save at +2, half damage from magical fire even on fail)
Resistance to cold (same as fire resistance)
Darkness 1/day (as Darkness 15' Radius spell)
+2 to saves vs. fire and cold
Pass without trace 1/day
Detect magic 3/day (as spell)
special_disadvantages: Obvious fiendish heritage causes -2 to reaction with most NPCs
Holy water deals 1d4 damage on contact
Cannot multi-class
tiefling.alignment_restriction: Any non-good alignment; most are neutral or evil

githzerai.description: Gaunt humanoids from the chaotic plane of Limbo; former slaves of mind flayers
special_abilities: Psionic ability: Wild talent (50% chance; roll for power if yes)
+4 to saves vs. mind control and charm spells
Immune to mind flayer's Mind Blast on save vs. spell at +4
Telekinesis 1/day (as Clairsentience devotion, 5 lbs)
Feather fall 1/day
special_disadvantages: Cannot receive beneficial cleric spells from evil-aligned gods
Githyanki consider them enemies — automatic hostile reaction

githyanki.description: Militaristic humanoids from the Astral Plane; former slaves of mind flayers who rebelled
special_abilities: Psionic ability: Wild talent (65% chance)
+3 to saves vs. mind control
Astral projection 1/day (as devotion, self only)
Telekinesis 1/day (20 lbs)
Mage spells: at level 3 gain Magic Missile 1/day; at 6: Blur 1/day; at 9: Dimension Door 1/day
special_disadvantages: Ruthless and militaristic; -4 reaction with most non-Githyanki
Githzerai are their enemies — automatic hostile reaction
Must return to Astral Plane periodically or lose powers (1 month)

genasi_fire.description: Half-humans with elemental fire heritage; born from fire creature ancestry
special_abilities: Fire resistance: immune to normal fire; +4 to saves vs. magical fire; half damage from magical fire
Produce Flame 1/day
Detect magic related to fire at will
Low-light vision in firelight (see normally by campfire)
special_disadvantages: Vulnerability to cold: -2 to saves vs. cold; +1 damage per die from cold attacks
Hair that resembles flames (unmistakable)

special_abilities: Levitate 1/day (self only, as spell)
Wind walk 1/day (move at double speed for 1 turn)
+2 to saves vs. gas and air-based attacks
Never knocked back by wind
special_disadvantages: Slightly translucent appearance
-1 to saves vs. earth-based attacks

special_abilities: Move through stone and earth 1/day for 1 turn (as Earth Walk psionic power)
Tremor sense: feel vibrations through ground, detect movement within 30 ft
+2 to saves vs. earth-based attacks
Never falls prone from earth tremors
special_disadvantages: Cannot swim; always sink
Slow movement
-1 to saves vs. air-based attacks

special_abilities: Breathe water freely
Water breathing (can survive indefinitely underwater)
+2 to saves vs. water and cold attacks
Swim at movement 15; never fatigued by swimming
special_disadvantages: Slightly bluish skin
Slightly vulnerable to fire (-1 save vs. fire)
Must immerse in water once per week or lose special abilities for 1 week

dark_sun_races.description: Races from the Dark Sun (Athas) Campaign Setting — a dying world of heat and survival

mul.description: Dwarf-human hybrid; bred specifically as slave laborers on Athas. Incredibly tough and tireless.
special_abilities: Cannot be tired: rest only 1 night per week; can work 36 hours without rest penalty
Immune to heat exhaustion (Athasian heat is no penalty)
+4 to saves vs. exhaustion and disease
Sterile: Muls cannot reproduce

thri_kreen.description: Four-armed insectoid hunters; natural killing machines with chitin armor
special_abilities: Four arms: can hold 4 weapons simultaneously
Natural armor AC 5 (chitin)
Claw attack: 2 x 1d4+STR bonus per round (in addition to weapons)
Bite: 1d4+2 with paralyzing venom (save vs. poison or paralyzed 1d6 rounds)
Cannot be surprised (360-degree compound eye vision)
Jump: leap 20 ft horizontal, 10 ft vertical from standing
No sleep required: ever
special_disadvantages: Cannot wear human armor (body shape incompatible)
Considered monsters by most humanoid civilizations
Cannot easily use standard equipment designed for bipeds
Alien mindset: -1 to all Charisma-based checks with non-insectoid beings

complete_book_of_humanoids_races.description: Races from the Complete Book of Humanoids (PHBR10) — available for players to use with DM permission

bugbear.description: Large, shaggy goblinoid race; stealthy and powerful
special_abilities: Surprise on 3-in-6 despite size (naturally quiet movement)
Natural strength bonus applies to all attacks
+1 to hide in shadows checks (dark coloring)
Bite attack: 1d4+STR bonus in addition to weapons
Scent: detect by smell within 30 ft; track by scent
+2 to saves vs. disease

goblin.description: Small, cunning humanoids; excellent at finding and using traps
special_abilities: +2 to find traps
+1 to move silently in natural environments
Small size: +4 AC vs. large creatures
Ride wolves and worgs at full effectiveness

hobgoblin.description: Disciplined, militaristic humanoids; better organized than other goblinoids
special_abilities: Military training: proficient with any 3 weapons from level 1
+1 to all attack rolls when fighting in formation with other hobgoblins
Natural leaders: +1 morale to goblinoid troops under command

kobold.description: Tiny, scaly humanoids; expert trappers and miners
special_abilities: Trap expertise: +20% to find and set traps
Mining sense: detect slopes, construction, etc. as PHB dwarves
Tiny size: +6 AC vs. large creatures
Light sensitivity: -1 to attack in daylight (compensated by infravision)

lizard_man.description: Reptilian humanoids; powerful swimmers and natural warriors
special_abilities: Natural swimmer: swim at movement 12; never tire in water
Claw/claw/bite attack: 1d2/1d2/1d4 (in addition to weapons)
Camouflage in swamp: 60% concealment when stationary
Hold breath 2 rounds per CON point
Natural armor: scales provide AC 6 base (can still wear armor)

ogre.description: Huge brutes; rare player race with significant drawbacks
special_abilities: Natural attack: 1d10 punch damage (in addition to weapons)
Huge size: can carry 300 lbs without encumbrance
Intimidation: -4 to morale of opponents less than half ogre size when first seen
special_disadvantages: Must eat 5x normal food
Equipment requires custom sizing (2-3x normal cost)
Cannot use standard horses or small boats
Treated with extreme suspicion in civilized areas

special_abilities: Rage: 1/day, +2 STR and +2 CON for 3 rounds; afterward -1 to each for 1 hour
Darkvision 60 ft
Natural survivalists: Survival proficiency (any terrain) for free at 1st level
special_disadvantages: Light sensitivity: -1 to attacks in bright daylight
-4 to reaction with most civilized humanoids
Cannot take Paladin class

al_qadim_races.description: Races from the Al-Qadim Campaign Setting — an Arabian Nights-inspired setting

asherati.description: Desert-dwelling humanoids who swim through sand; native to the deep desert
special_abilities: Swim through sand at movement 9 as easily as walking
Breathe air and sand equally
Sand sight: see through sandstorms
+4 to all saves vs. heat and desert conditions
Detect water within 500 ft

spelljammer_races.description: Races encountered in the Spelljammer setting — space-faring races

hadozee.description: Simian race with gliding membranes; excellent ship crew members
special_abilities: Glide: extend membranes between arms and legs to glide from any height; take no falling damage
Climb at full movement rate
Prehensile feet: hold objects or swing from ropes with feet
+4 to all climbing and rope use checks

giff.description: Hippopotamus-headed militaristic humanoids; obsessed with firearms and explosives
special_abilities: Headbutt attack: 2d6 (in addition to weapons)
+2 to all firearm and explosive attacks and damage
Never stunned by explosions (deaf for 1 round only)
Never frightened by combat or battle sounds

rare_demi_human_subraces.description: Non-standard demi-human subraces beyond the PHB races

gray_elf.description: Ancient high elves; more intelligent but more aloof than standard elves
special_abilities: All standard elf abilities
+1 to INT-based proficiency checks
May learn 10th-level spells (unlike other elves)
Longevity: age at 1/10 the rate of standard elves
special_disadvantages: CON -2 (fragile)
Extremely arrogant: -2 to reaction with non-elves

wild_elf.description: Primitive forest elves; strong but less civilized than standard elves
special_abilities: All standard elf abilities
+3 to any wilderness survival checks
Move silently at +15% (natural stealth)
+1 to hit with natural weapons (knives, spears, bows)

drow.description: Dark elves exiled to the Underdark; powerful but reviled by surface races
special_abilities: 120-ft infravision
Innate magic: Dancing Lights, Faerie Fire, Darkness 1/day each
+2 to saves vs. magic and poison
Detect magic passively (no activation required)
Move silently at +20%
special_disadvantages: Light blindness: -2 to attack, saves, and proficiency checks in bright sunlight or Light spell
Drow equipment degrades in sunlight: degrades in 1 week above ground
Surface races treat drow with extreme suspicion (-4 to most reactions)
Available only in campaigns where surface contact is allowed by DM

mountain_dwarf.description: Standard PHB dwarf variant; focused on metalworking and mountain warfare
differences_from_standard: +1 to attack with hammers and axes (their primary weapons)
+3 to saves vs. breath weapon instead of standard dwarf bonus
Mine sense extends to 100 ft instead of standard 50 ft

deep_gnome.description: Svirfneblin; gnomes adapted to the Underdark
special_abilities: 120-ft infravision
Non-detection (as spell, always active)
Blur (as spell) 1/day
Blindness (as spell) 1/day
Change Self 1/day
+2 to saves vs. spells, wands, and staves
+2 to saves vs. poison
special_disadvantages: Light sensitivity: -2 to attack in sunlight
Very rare; mistrusted even by surface gnomes
